篷.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 逢: a bamboo-frame cover, so 篷 means an awning or tent canopy.

sentence.en: We pitch a tent on the grass.

簇.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 族: things gathered together, so 簇 means a cluster or bunch.

sentence.en: A cluster of flowers has bloomed.

簸.origin: ⺮ (bamboo) over 其 and 皮: a bamboo tray for tossing grain to clean it, a winnowing pan.

sentence.en: She uses a dustpan for the trash.

米.origin: Looks like scattered grains: 米 means uncooked rice.

sentence.en: We bought a bag of rice.

籴.origin: 入 (enter) over 米 (grain): bringing grain in, so 籴 means to buy grain.

sentence.en: He went to the market to buy rice.

粘.origin: 米 (rice) plus 占: cooked rice is sticky, so 粘 means to stick or sticky.

sentence.en: Stick the photo onto the notebook.

粟.origin: 覀 over 米 (grain): 粟 is millet, a small grain crop.

sentence.en: Ancient people grew millet.

粤.origin: Holds 米 inside; 粤 stands for Guangdong and the Cantonese language.

sentence.en: He can speak Cantonese.

粥.origin: 米 (rice) between two 弓 shapes like rising steam: 粥 means porridge or congee.

sentence.en: In the morning I have a bowl of porridge.

粪.origin: 米 (grain) over 共: what is swept away after eating, so 粪 means dung or manure.

sentence.en: The farmer fertilizes the field with manure.

粽.origin: 米 (rice) plus 宗: 粽 is the sticky-rice dumpling wrapped in leaves.

sentence.en: We eat zongzi at the Dragon Boat Festival.

糊.origin: 米 (rice) plus 胡: rice cooked into a paste; 糊 also means muddled or blurry.

sentence.en: I am a little confused.

糕.origin: 米 (rice) plus 羔: 糕 means cake or pastry made from rice flour.

sentence.en: There is a cake for the birthday.

糖.origin: 米 (rice) plus 唐: sweet stuff often made from grain, so 糖 means sugar or candy.

sentence.en: Children love eating candy.

糙.origin: 米 (rice) plus 造: unpolished rice feels rough, so 糙 means coarse or rough.

sentence.en: This board is very rough.

糜.origin: 麻 over 米 (rice): rice boiled soft, so 糜 means rice gruel.

sentence.en: Gruel is cooking in the pot.

糟.origin: 米 (grain) plus 曹: leftover mash from brewing, so 糟 means dregs; it also means a mess.

sentence.en: It is raining, how terrible.

糠.origin: 米 (rice) plus 康: the dry husk around the grain, so 糠 means bran or chaff.

sentence.en: Rice bran can feed the chickens.

素.origin: Plain silk thread 糸 below: 素 means plain, simple, or vegetarian.

sentence.en: She dresses very plainly.

索.origin: A rope (糸) hanging down: 索 means a thick rope or to search for.

sentence.en: He grabbed the rope and climbed up.

紧.origin: Silk thread 糸 pulled hard below: 紧 means tight or tense.

sentence.en: I am nervous before the exam.

絮.origin: 如 over 糸 (silk): soft fluffy fibers, so 絮 means cotton padding or fluff.

sentence.en: There is cotton padding inside the quilt.

纠.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 丩: threads twisted together, so 纠 means tangled or to correct.

sentence.en: The teacher corrects my wrong characters.

纤.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 千: very thin threads, so 纤 means fine or slender.

sentence.en: There is fiber in vegetables.

级.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 及: steps measured like knots on a thread, so 级 means level or grade.

sentence.en: I am in the third grade.

纯.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 屯: silk with nothing added, so 纯 means pure.

sentence.en: This is pure water.

纱.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 少: thin loose threads, so 纱 means gauze or yarn.

sentence.en: The nurse wraps the wound with gauze.

纳.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 内: bringing thread in, so 纳 means to accept or take in.

sentence.en: The teacher accepted my suggestion.

纵.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 从: a thread let loose, so 纵 means to let go or lengthwise.

sentence.en: The roads crisscross each other.

纹.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 文 (pattern): thread-like lines, so 纹 means a line or stripe.

sentence.en: This cloth has a pretty pattern.

纺.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 方: making thread, so 纺 means to spin.

sentence.en: Grandma can spin thread.

纽.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 丑: a thread tied into a knot, so 纽 means a button or knot.

sentence.en: There are five buttons on the shirt.

组.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 且: threads joined together, so 组 means a group or to organize.

sentence.en: We split into four small groups.

织.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 只: working with thread, so 织 means to weave or knit.

sentence.en: Mom is knitting a sweater.

绊.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 半: thread that trips you up, so 绊 means to trip or stumble.

sentence.en: He tripped over a stone.

经.origin: 纟 (thread): the lengthwise threads of weaving; 经 also means classic books and to pass through.

word.en: to pass by, to go through

sentence.en: I pass by the park every day.

绑.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 邦: using cord, so 绑 means to tie or bind.

sentence.en: Please help me tie my shoelaces.

绒.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 戎: soft fine threads, so 绒 means velvet, fluff, or wool.

sentence.en: The duckling has soft down on its body.

绕.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 尧: winding thread around, so 绕 means to wind or go around.

sentence.en: We run around the playground.

绗.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 行: stitching long lines through cloth, so 绗 means to quilt.

sentence.en: Grandma is quilting the blanket.

绘.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 会: like embroidering a picture, so 绘 means to draw or paint.

sentence.en: I like to draw.

络.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 各: a mesh of threads, so 络 means a net or to connect.

sentence.en: I use the internet to look up information.

绞.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 交: threads crossing, so 绞 means to twist or wring.

sentence.en: She wrung the towel dry.

绢.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 肙: 绢 is a smooth, thin kind of silk.

sentence.en: She has a red handkerchief.

绣.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 秀: making something beautiful with thread, so 绣 means to embroider.

sentence.en: She is embroidering on the cloth.

绪.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 者: the loose end of a thread; 绪 means the beginnings or one's mood.

sentence.en: He is in a good mood today.

绱.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 尚: sewing the sole onto a shoe, so 绱 means to attach a sole.

sentence.en: The cobbler is attaching the sole.

绲.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 昆: a woven band, so 绲 means a cord or sash.

sentence.en: The trim on the clothes is very neat.

绳.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 黾: many threads twisted thick, so 绳 means rope or cord.

sentence.en: We jump rope with the rope.

维.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 隹: threads holding things together, so 维 means to maintain or hold.

sentence.en: We should protect the environment.
